sources: Australian Government Department of Health, Disability and Ageing — Average annual price changes in private health insurance premiums (industry-weighted average, annual premium rounds)
CHOICE (Feb 2026) — 'Insurers hiding soaring increases to top-level health cover' (5-year Gold vs industry-average comparison, Jan 2021 to Apr 2025)
CHOICE (Mar 2026) — 'Health insurance price hikes higher than ever' (big-five per-tier breakdown for the April 2025 and April 2026 rounds)
CHOICE (Feb 2024) — 'Health insurers are increasing their top-level policy prices by over 30%' (3-year Gold by fund, Feb 2021 to Feb 2024)
meta.notes: Per-tier premium increases are not published by the Department of Health in a machine-readable form. Only the April 2025 and April 2026 premium rounds have credible public per-tier estimates (CHOICE analysis of the big-five insurers: Bupa, HCF, HBF, Medibank, NIB). Multi-year Gold-vs-industry totals come from separate CHOICE investigations of new-customer policies. The industry-average series for every year is DoH's approved weighted average.

multi_year_observations.note: National average for Gold hospital policies available to new customers; compared with DoH-published industry-weighted average across all hospital and extras policies over the same window.

multi_year_observations.window_label: Feb 2021 to Feb 2024 (3-year, by big-five fund)
multi_year_observations.note: Gold hospital policies available to new customers at each of the big-five funds; industry-average figure is the DoH cumulative across the same window.
